name: Content Publisher

releases.author: roomi-fields
releases.description:
## Obsidian Community Plugin Store scan fixes

Addresses all 294 findings from the Community Plugin Store scan.

### Fixed

- **Type safety**: Eliminated ~120 `no-unsafe-*` errors. API responses and frontmatter access now flow through `Record<string, unknown>` with type guards; `String.prototype.replace` callbacks are explicitly typed.
- **Popout window compatibility**: `document` → `activeDocument`, `setTimeout`/`setInterval` → `activeWindow.*`, `btoa` via `activeWindow`, `document.createDocumentFragment()` → `createFragment()`.
- **Unused arg**: `filename` → `_filename` in Substack `uploadImage`.
- **Dead CSS**: Removed unused rules with `!important`.
- **README**: Dropped "Coming Soon" placeholder — the plugin is now in the store.
- **i18n**: Translated remaining French strings in batch publish notices.

### Added

- **Release attestation**: Release assets now ship with a GitHub-signed SLSA provenance attestation via `actions/attest-build-provenance@v2`.
- **Scan reproducibility**: New `eslint.scan.config.js` reproduces the directory scanner output locally.

### Changed

- `npm audit fix` cleared all vulnerable transitive devDeps (postcss, rollup, yaml). Vitest stays at 3.2.4 because v4 requires Node 20+.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
releases.name: v2.4.0

releases.author: roomi-fields

releases.author: roomi-fields
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Address all Obsidian Community Plugin Store review issues (88 sentence case violations, inline styles, fetch/requestUrl, async onOpen, console statements, vault.delete, undescribed directives, unused imports)
- ESLint config updated with custom brands/acronyms for sentence case rule
- Replace all inline styles with CSS classes for better theming
- Refactor Modal.onOpen() to void wrapper pattern in all PostComposer modals

releases.author: roomi-fields
releases.description: Obsidian Community Plugin Store review fixes

releases.author: roomi-fields
releases.description:
## What's New

### TikZ → PNG Conversion
- Render TikZ diagrams to PNG images for WordPress (tikzjax → SVG → Canvas → PNG)
- Inline @font-face rules into SVG for correct font rendering
- Support both 3 and 4 backtick tikz blocks
- Stabilize SVG capture with DOM mutation debouncing (2s)
- Fix CRLF line endings on Windows

### LaTeX Text → Unicode
- Convert LaTeX text-mode commands to Unicode in publish pipeline (\'{e} → é, \guillemotleft → «)
- Protect math regions ($...$, $$...$$) from conversion — KaTeX handles those
- Fix inline code regex to prevent cross-line matching (root cause of blockquote bugs)

### Other
- Transparent PNG background for TikZ diagrams
- Console logging for TikZ rendering progress

releases.author: roomi-fields
releases.description:
## LinkedIn Publishing Support

Full LinkedIn integration is now available!

### Added

- **LinkedIn Publishing** — Full LinkedIn integration
  - Text posts and shared article posts with link preview
  - Auto-select "Shared Article" when `wordpress_url` or `substack_url` exists in frontmatter
  - Editable preview before publishing (3000 character limit)
  - Draft support (save as draft before publishing)
  - Bilingual FR/EN support
  - Step-by-step OAuth setup guide in settings with Postman instructions
  - Test connection validates Person ID

### Changed

- LinkedIn post preview is now editable before publishing
- Blockquotes (`>`) preserved as-is in LinkedIn posts
- Settings opens external links in system default browser

### Fixed

- LinkedIn Person ID validation in test connection
- Article URL auto-population from frontmatter

---

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
releases.name: v2.2.0 - LinkedIn Publishing

releases.author: roomi-fields
releases.description:
## Drop Cap Images (Enluminures) & Wikilinks

### Added

- **Drop Cap Images (Enluminures)** — Medieval manuscript-style decorative initial images
  - Auto-detect images with "enluminure" in path
  - Float left as visual drop cap
  - Set as featured image and Open Graph image
- **Wikilinks Conversion** — Automatic conversion to WordPress internal links
  - `[[Page Name]]` → WordPress post link
  - `[[Page Name|Display Text]]` → Custom link text

### Changed

- Improved image upload handling
- Better error messages for WordPress API

---

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
releases.name: v2.1.0 - Drop Cap Images & Wikilinks

releases.author: roomi-fields
releases.description:
## WordPress Multi-Server & Polylang Bilingual Support

### Added

- **WordPress Multi-Server** — Configure multiple WordPress sites (production, staging, etc.)
- **Server Selector** — Choose which server to publish to in the modal
- **Polylang Bilingual Publishing** — Publish FR and EN versions simultaneously
  - Language-specific category mappings
  - Automatic Polylang language tags
  - Linked translations

### Changed

- Settings reorganized with server management UI
- Improved WordPress API error handling

---

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
releases.name: v2.0.0 - WordPress Multi-Server & Bilingual

releases.author: roomi-fields
releases.description:
## What's New

### ✨ Features

- **YAML Frontmatter Support** - Set `title`, `subtitle`, `audience`, `tags`, and `section` directly in your note's frontmatter
- **Audience Selector** - Target specific readers: everyone, paid-only, free-only, or founding members
- **Tags Support** - Add tags via frontmatter or in the publish modal
- **Sections Support** - Select publication sections (auto-fetched from your Substack)
- **Auto-detect Paid Subscriptions** - Plugin detects if your publication has paid subscriptions enabled
- **Default Settings** - Configure default publication, section, audience, and tags in plugin settings
- **Refresh Button** - Sync publications and sections from Substack with one click

### 🔧 Changes

- Settings UI reorganized with Authentication, Defaults, and Advanced sections
- Ribbon icon now positioned at bottom of ribbon
- Test coverage improved to 43.65% (173 tests)

### 🐛 Fixes

- ESLint sentence-case compliance for all UI text
- Removed unused imports and debug code

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` and place them in your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-substack/` folder.
releases.name: v1.2.0 - Frontmatter, Audience, Tags & Sections

releases.author: roomi-fields
releases.description:
## 🖼️ Local Image Upload

This release adds automatic local image upload support! Images in your notes are now uploaded to Substack CDN when publishing.

### Features

- **Automatic upload** - Local images (`![alt](image.png)`) are uploaded to Substack CDN
- **Format support** - PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF, WebP
- **Path resolution** - Handles relative paths (`../images/photo.png`), absolute vault paths (`/attachments/img.png`)
- **Error handling** - Clear warnings if images fail to upload, post continues without them

### How it works

1. Write your note with local images: `![photo](./images/photo.png)`
2. Click Publish to Substack
3. Images are automatically uploaded and paths replaced with CDN URLs
4. Your post publishes with all images!

### Technical

- 161 tests (41 new)
- 52.7% code coverage
- New `ImageHandler` class
- Uses Substack's base64 data URI upload format

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/roomi-fields/obsidian-substack/compare/v1.0.7...v1.1.0
releases.name: v1.1.0 - Local Image Upload

releases.author: roomi-fields
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Added
- 🧪 **Codecov Integration** - Coverage reports now uploaded to Codecov on every CI run
- 📊 **README Badges** - CI status and Codecov coverage badges
- ✅ **36 New Unit Tests** - Test suite expanded from 84 to 120 tests
- 🔧 **New Dev Dependencies** - Added Prettier and type-coverage

### Changed
- 📈 **Improved Test Coverage** - Overall coverage increased from 38% to 47%
  - `api.ts`: 68% → 100%
  - `logger.ts`: 34% → 83%
- ⚙️ **ESLint Config** - Now properly supports `.cjs`, `.mjs`, and test files

### Fixed
- 🐛 ESLint errors in Node.js build scripts (console, __dirname globals)
- 🔒 Security vulnerabilities in dependencies
- 💅 Code formatting with Prettier

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/roomi-fields/obsidian-substack/compare/v1.0.6...v1.0.7

releases.author: roomi-fields
releases.description:
## 🎉 First Release!

Publish your Obsidian notes directly to Substack.

### Features

- **🔐 One-Click Login** — Automatic Substack authentication (desktop only)
- **📝 Draft & Publish** — Save as draft or publish immediately
- **📚 Multi-Publication** — Manage multiple Substack publications
- **🔄 Markdown Conversion** — Full conversion to Substack format
  - Headers (H1-H6)
  - Bold, italic, links, code
  - Ordered/unordered lists
  - Code blocks with syntax
  - Blockquotes
  - Horizontal rules

### Installation

1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below
2. Create folder: `.obsidian/plugins/substack-publisher/`
3. Copy files into the folder
4. Restart Obsidian and enable the plugin

### Setup

1. Open plugin settings → Click "Login"
2. Sign in to Substack in the popup
3. Add your publication subdomain(s)
4. Start publishing!

---

See [README](https://github.com/roomi-fields/obsidian-substack#readme) for full documentation.
